Title at top: “Cytoarchitectonic changes in occipital lobe”

Main diagram: pink rectangular panel with a histological-looking cortical cross-section drawn in dark red/brown dots and outlines, showing folded cortex.

Labels and arrows:
- “Junction” above the upper middle boundary between two cortical regions, black downward arrow pointing to the boundary.
- “Area 18” at upper left inside the pink panel.
- “Area 17 (striate cortex)” at upper right inside the pink panel.
- Small black upward arrow near the center of the section, pointing upward within the pale inner region bordered by cortex.

Structure depicted:
- Left side labeled Area 18: cortical ribbon forming a fold, with layered dotted texture.
- Right side labeled Area 17 (striate cortex): thicker cortical ribbon on the right fold, more densely dotted/layered.
- Boundary between the two regions located near the top center under “Junction.”
- Pale interior spaces enclosed by the cortical folds.
- Additional partial cortical folds visible at lower left and lower right edges of the panel.

Title: “Cytoarchitectonic changes in occipital lobe”

Main diagram: histological cortical section with two labeled regions and a boundary.
- Top center label: “Junction” with a downward arrow pointing to the boundary at the crest of a gyrus.
- Left upper label: “Area 18”
- Right upper label: “Area 17 (striate cortex)”

Diagram details:
- Folded cortical ribbon shown in cross-section, pink background.
- Outer cortical contour undulating, with a prominent gyrus/sulcus pattern.
- Dense stippled cellular texture throughout cortex.
- Right side (Area 17) appears thicker and more densely granular than left side (Area 18).
- A dark band within the cortex is especially prominent in Area 17, curving along the cortical ribbon; consistent with the stria of Gennari.
- Left side cortex (Area 18) has less prominent internal banding.
- White matter/core regions are pale and relatively unstippled.
- A black upward arrow inside the central pale region points toward the inner cortical surface near the middle of the section.
